columns.name: asset_name
columns.description: The specific digital asset where the finding was detected (e.g., a domain, subdomain, IP address, or URL).

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The UTC timestamp when this event was ingested into Microsoft Sentinel. Use this for time-range filtering in KQL

columns.name: category
columns.description: The high-level security domain of the finding (Possible values: Human, Network and IT, Application)

columns.name: cves
columns.description: A list of Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) IDs associated with this finding, if applicable (e.g., [CVE-2024-44228]).

columns.name: description
columns.description: A detailed explanation of the vulnerability, including why it is a risk and the potential impact

columns.name: evidence
columns.description: Technical proof collected during the scan. May include HTTP headers, open port nr., or specific configuration settings that triggered the finding.

columns.name: finding_text
columns.description: The headline or short title of the specific finding (e.g., Cloud Private Services Exposed)

columns.name: id
columns.description: The unique identifier for this specific finding within the Panorays platform.

columns.name: is_hidden
columns.description: Indicates if this finding has been manually suppressed or hidden in the Panorays dashboard (true = hidden)

columns.name: metadata
columns.description: A JSON object containing additional context specific to the finding type (e.g., specific port numbers, certificate details)

columns.name: previously_disputed
columns.description: Flag indicating if this finding was previously marked as a false positive or disputed by an analyst.

columns.name: segments
columns.description: A list of business units, tags, or asset groups within Panorays that this finding is associated with

columns.name: severity
columns.description: The risk level assigned to this finding. Possible values: Critical, High, Medium, Low, Info.

columns.name: status
columns.description: The current lifecycle state of the finding. Possible values Open, Done, Closed.

columns.name: status_update_ts
columns.description: The timestamp (as a string) reflecting specifically when the status field last changed.

columns.name: sub_category
columns.description: A granular classification of the finding within the parent category (e.g., Exposed Services, Mail Server etc.).

columns.name: test_name
columns.description: test name

columns.name: test_text
columns.description: test text

columns.name: update_ts
columns.description: The timestamp (UTC) when the record was last modified in the Panorays.

columns.name: updated_by
columns.description: The user or system process that performed the last update to this record.
